activation_results.reason: Input is the canonical description verbatim.
Review is a direct synonym for Audit and the rest matches the description.
User explicitly invokes the skill by its kebab-case name.
SSH key forwarding on Windows is unrelated to CI/CD pipeline auditing.
Generic meta question about skills, not a request to audit pipelines.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Uses 'artefact', 'artefacts' and 'authorised'-style spellings; no American forms detected.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Risk Register and per-workflow tables show file:line (e.g., release.yml:17, deploy-prod.yml:33) with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM severity columns.
Contains Executive Summary, Category Scores, Per-Workflow Findings, Cross-Workflow Patterns, Risk Register, Runtime Baseline, Action Batches, Suppressed Findings.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Names concrete target 'acme-api' and real workflow paths like .github/workflows/release.yml and deploy-prod.yml; no placeholders.

judge_result.notes: All four judge criteria are satisfied by the example artefact. The criterion listing canonical sections is truncated in the prompt ('Report includes the canonical sections:' with no explicit list), so it was evaluated against the conventional audit-report sections, all of which are present. Verdict set to 'partial' only because the third criterion is under-specified in the prompt; on the evidence available the artefact fully meets it.
